hooks.command: echo 'RESUME (contradictionchecker, as of 2026-05-30). State: PRs #60-#67 all merged to main; corpus isolation v1 + v1.1 follow-up bugs done; regression coverage in tests/test_corpus_isolation_v1_1.py. --corpus is REQUIRED on ingest/check/estimate-cost/export/store reidentify-orgs (interactive picker on TTY; consistency-check corpus list to see what is there). Judge/extractor default to Moonshot/Kimi (kimi-k2.6); needs MOONSHOT_API_KEY in gitignored .env; run from repo root. Atkins corpus ingest done (8645 assertions from 2 of 5 PDFs; other 3 are scanned images that pypdf extracts to empty string and add_document collapsed into one sentinel doc_id row); check stage paused per project_atkins_corpus_2026-05-30.md (estimate $101-339 on OpenAI pricing, ~$3-30 realistic on Moonshot). Competitive intel (verified via WebSearch 2026-05-30): Definely Cascade (launched Aug 2025) is the direct competitor — embedding+LLM detection of contract-edit knock-on effects, lives in MS Word, single-contract scope, drafting-time. Spellbook Associate = multi-doc Q&A across deal sets. Luminance Auto-Review = template-deviation flagging. The surviving wedge Cascade does NOT cover is the BACKWARD AUDIT lane: static corpus nobody is currently editing (DD lawyers inheriting a target contract book; compliance reviewing handbooks they did not author; non-legal verticals like policy/regs/technical docs). Top commercial blockers if pursued: (1) OCR for scanned PDFs (Atkins proved 60% of a real legal corpus silently failed), (2) publish real CONTRADOC precision/recall on README, (3) --max-cost hard ceiling + default estimates to Moonshot pricing, (4) flip pairwise detector to opt-in (own eval 2026-05-21 shows near-zero yield on legal prose; definition detector is the lever), (5) PyPI release + pipx launcher. Brand split (repo contradictionchecker / package consistency-checker / product CrossCheck) should consolidate. Full background in MEMORY.md.'
